head: e069e3a9c758891f5a15e6dadd550be6dbacb326

commits.subject: feat(R-20-NV): schema step 22 — canonical_nodes validity window (SCHEMA 21→22)
feat(R-20-RV/R-20-NV): thread ReadView through all five read verbs + bindings
test(R-20-RV/R-20-NV): read-mode + validity matrices; Py/TS parity; surface delta
docs(rust): record the Slice-10b ReadView/BoundaryCrossing surface delta

gates.note: Real exit codes captured via $? on the command itself, never a trailing echo. Test suites were run SERIALLY (--test-threads=1) per the lifecycle_observability.rs false-failure warning; no parallel suite run was performed. 540 tests passed across 102 suites.

api_shape_decision.chosen: thread a `ReadView` parameter through the five existing read verbs
api_shape_decision.rationale: Governed-surface minimisation. Sibling verbs would have added roughly eight new allowlist members across the Py/TS naming conventions (read.get_with_view / readGetWithView, and so on for five verbs); threading adds ZERO new verb names on the read path. The net delta is TWO TYPES (`ReadView`, `BoundaryCrossing`) plus ONE genuinely new command (`crossed_boundary_since`, which has no existing verb to ride on). 0.8.20 is already breaking on the Rust facade, and in both bindings the parameter is OPTIONAL (`view=None` / `view?`), so neither SDK is source-broken. Cost: ~60 internal/test call sites updated to pass `&ReadView::default()` — mechanical, and it has the side benefit that every pre-existing read-verb test now explicitly exercises the strict view.

integer_vs_text_divergence.flag: DELIBERATE DIVERGENCE — ESCALATED TO HITL, NOT SILENTLY RESOLVED
integer_vs_text_divergence.what: `canonical_nodes.valid_from` / `valid_until` are INTEGER epoch seconds. The shipped `canonical_edges.t_valid` / `t_invalid` (migration step 14) are ISO-8601 TEXT compared through `datetime(...)`. The temporal model is now internally inconsistent between nodes and edges.
why: the R-20-NV release contract specifies INTEGER windows
an INTEGER window stays sargable against `canonical_nodes_validity_idx` with no per-row `datetime()` conversion
INTEGER lets the instant be a BOUND parameter (the `:now` seam), which is what makes node validity deterministically testable; the shipped EDGE path still inlines `datetime('now')`
unifying would either re-litigate shipped edge semantics or force a data rewrite of `canonical_edges` — both out of scope for this slice
integer_vs_text_divergence.not_done: The edge columns were NOT changed, NOT unified, and NOT rewritten. Pinned by `s22_edge_temporal_columns_are_untouched_text` and `r_20_nv_edge_temporal_filter_is_unchanged` so the divergence is a recorded decision rather than drift.
integer_vs_text_divergence.recommendation: HITL should decide whether the node/edge temporal split is acceptable long-term or whether a future slice unifies edges onto INTEGER. This slice does not presume that answer.

governed_surface_delta.recovery_denylist: UNCHANGED — exactly {recover, restore, repair, fix, rebuild}. No new name collides (nothing is named rebuild_*). AC-041 (`no_recovery_surface`) re-run GREEN.
governed_surface_delta.ac_minting: No new AC minted. Work is tracked by requirement id (R-20-RV / R-20-NV) + TDD test names, per the acceptance.md lock. AC-079 remains unminted and available.
governed_surface_delta.note: The phrase HITL-SIGNED was NOT written anywhere by this slice.

schema.convention: half-open [valid_from, valid_until); NULL = unbounded on that side
schema.index: canonical_nodes_validity_idx ON (valid_from, valid_until) WHERE superseded_at IS NULL AND state = 'active'
schema.crash_safety: rides the existing `apply_one` BEGIN IMMEDIATE wrapping SQL + PRAGMA user_version together; a failed step rolls back to 21 with BOTH columns absent (asserted by s22_failed_step_rolls_back_columns_and_version_together)
schema.idempotence: `migrate_with_event_sink` only applies steps with step_id > user_version — load-bearing because ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN has no IF NOT EXISTS form (asserted by s22_is_idempotent_across_repeated_migrate_calls)
schema.no_op_on_existing_data: existing rows back-fill to NULL/NULL ⇒ unbounded ⇒ valid at every instant ⇒ default-view visibility unchanged (asserted against the raw table at seven instants including i64::MIN/MAX)
version_pins_updated: fathomdb-schema/tests: step14, step15_to_17, step16, step17, step18, step19, step20, step21, migrations
fathomdb-engine/tests: compatibility.rs (step-id event list), vector_equivalence_probe.rs (upgrade head)

mutation_verification: Forced the BFS recursive-join node predicate to ignore the view (`ReadView::default()` instead of `view`): 3 RV tests FAILED, including `r_20_rv_graph_neighbors_view_applies_at_the_recursive_join` specifically. Restored and re-verified green.
Flipped the validity upper bound from `>` to `>=` (breaking the half-open convention): `r_20_nv_validity_window_matrix` FAILED. Restored and re-verified green.
Conclusion: neither suite is vacuously green.

uniform_application_evidence.note: The three per-direction BFS CTE copies (nine hand-written node predicates across anchor / recursive join / final projection) were folded into ONE template parameterised by the only two things that differ (edge join condition, traversed-to expression). Uniform application is therefore STRUCTURAL, not a thing to remember. Note the task brief said FOUR direction variants; there are in fact THREE `TraversalDirection` variants (Outgoing/Incoming/Both) — the fourth CTE is `build_bfs_with_depth_sql` for `search_expand`, which is NOT one of the five read verbs and was deliberately left on the strict path (unchanged behaviour).

not_done: NO write-side surface for AUTHORING validity windows. R-20-NV specifies the columns, the read-side selector and the boundary hook; a write verb is not in the requirement and inventing one would grow the governed surface. Tests set windows via direct SQL on a closed DB. CONSEQUENCE: validity is not yet end-to-end usable from an SDK — a consumer can query by window but cannot set one. This is the single most likely follow-up and is flagged for HITL, not silently assumed.

history_as_of / transaction-time: NOT built, not even partially. Pinned absent at the schema level (s22_adds_no_transaction_time_substrate) and at the type level (r_20_nv_read_view_has_no_transaction_time_selector, an exhaustive struct destructuring that stops compiling if a fifth ReadView field appears).
search / search_expand paths were NOT given a ReadView. They are not among the five read verbs, and touching them would risk ranking/scoring drift.
canonical_edges temporal columns and the edge `datetime('now')` filter: untouched by design.
Slices 15 / 20 / 25 / 30 / 40 (projection registry, EAV, etc.): not started.
TC-11 (h: pin A) untouched. resolve_provenance, source_id and Slice-10a work untouched.
Ranking, scoring, ordering and search-hit shape untouched.
eu7: ZERO runs on any backend at any N. `eu7_real_corpus_ac.rs` is byte-identical to baseline (git diff vs 43ae248f is empty) and still #[ignore]d.
caveats_for_reviewer: Python and TypeScript could NOT be built or executed in this worktree (no node_modules; maturin develop / pip install -e are forbidden here because they rebind the shared .venv). The Rust halves of both bindings compile and clippy clean under `cargo check/clippy --workspace --all-targets`, but the .py / .ts / .pyi edits are UNEXECUTED. They need a fresh-clone run before any green claim on X1 parity.

`ReadView::default()` reproduces the pre-slice EXISTENCE predicates exactly, and every pre-existing read-verb suite passes unchanged. It is not literally byte-identical SQL, because R-20-NV requires the validity conjunct with valid_as_of 'defaulting to now' — that conjunct is always emitted in the default view. It is a PROVABLE no-op on any row with an unbounded (NULL/NULL) window, which is every row in every database predating step 22. Both facts are asserted. Calling this out explicitly because the brief phrased RV's constraint as 'byte-identical' while NV's requirement mandates the always-on conjunct; the two are reconciled in favour of behavioural identity on existing data, and a caller who wants the conjunct gone entirely can set include_out_of_window.

`read_get` / `read_get_many` under include_superseded resolve a multi-match logical_id to the HIGHEST write_cursor (most recent version), via ORDER BY write_cursor. This is a new semantic choice the requirement did not specify; it is documented on the field and pinned by r_20_rv_point_lookup_under_include_superseded_is_deterministic.
